\doxysubsection{Detailed Description}
Some users would prefer to, instead of parsing a full string and walking a potentially huge \doxylink{sexp_8h_ae49130a807f3037c4b586b34006adcf2}{sexp\+\_\+t} structure, use an XML SAX-\/style parser where events are triggered as certain parts of the s-\/expression are encountered. This structure contains a set of function pointers that are called by the parser as it hits expression start and end, and completes reading atoms and binary data. NOTE\+: The parser\+\_\+event\+\_\+handler struct that is a field in the continuation data structure is NOT freed by destroy\+\_\+continuation since structs for callbacks are ALWAYS malloc\textquotesingle{}d by the user, not the library. 

\label{doc-variable-members}
\Hypertarget{structparser__event__handlers_doc-variable-members}
\doxysubsection{Field Documentation}
\Hypertarget{structparser__event__handlers_a8bb9528656e02c95d8c8d013a10baf08}\index{parser\_event\_handlers@{parser\_event\_handlers}!binary@{binary}}
\index{binary@{binary}!parser\_event\_handlers@{parser\_event\_handlers}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{binary}{binary}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{structparser__event__handlers_a8bb9528656e02c95d8c8d013a10baf08} 
void(\texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*} binary) (const char \texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}data, size\+\_\+t len)}

The binary function pointer is called when the parser is functioning in INLINE\+\_\+\+BINARY mode and binary data is encountered. The function must take two arguments\+: a pointer to the beginning of the binary data and the number of bytes of data present. \Hypertarget{structparser__event__handlers_ad0bfb2163406938507e9ff5ff811b2c1}\index{parser\_event\_handlers@{parser\_event\_handlers}!characters@{characters}}
\index{characters@{characters}!parser\_event\_handlers@{parser\_event\_handlers}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{characters}{characters}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{structparser__event__handlers_ad0bfb2163406938507e9ff5ff811b2c1} 
void(\texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*} characters) (const char \texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}data, size\+\_\+t len, \mbox{\hyperlink{sexp_8h_a60aa3e0d0ec7b4ce00dedb96e043c2e8}{atom\+\_\+t}} aty)}

The characters function pointer is called when an atom is completely parsed. The function must take three arguments\+: a pointer to the atom data, the number of elements the atom contains, and the specific type of atom that the data represents. \Hypertarget{structparser__event__handlers_a62872180b545e4d37fb39fed2e0db1a7}\index{parser\_event\_handlers@{parser\_event\_handlers}!end\_sexpr@{end\_sexpr}}
\index{end\_sexpr@{end\_sexpr}!parser\_event\_handlers@{parser\_event\_handlers}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{end\_sexpr}{end\_sexpr}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{structparser__event__handlers_a62872180b545e4d37fb39fed2e0db1a7} 
void(\texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*} end\+\_\+sexpr) ()}

The end\+\_\+sexpr function pointer is called when an close parenthesis is encountered ending an expression. \Hypertarget{structparser__event__handlers_a3600759a7f4ea3024d7ee83ec36962d7}\index{parser\_event\_handlers@{parser\_event\_handlers}!start\_sexpr@{start\_sexpr}}
\index{start\_sexpr@{start\_sexpr}!parser\_event\_handlers@{parser\_event\_handlers}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{start\_sexpr}{start\_sexpr}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{structparser__event__handlers_a3600759a7f4ea3024d7ee83ec36962d7} 
void(\texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*} start\+\_\+sexpr) ()}

The start\+\_\+sexpr function pointer is called when an open parenthesis is encountered starting an expression.
